description: The Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 marked a significant shift in US fiscal policy by lowering income tax rates. Public opinion was initially divided along partisan lines regarding the potential deficit impact versus the stimulus promise.

key_events.description: President Bush introduces the proposal in his first address to Congress.
Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan gives conditional support for tax rebates.
Senate Finance Committee moves the bill to a floor vote.
President Bush signs the $1.35 trillion package into law.

calibration_notes: Measures the impact of executive messaging combined with bipartisan compromise during economic uncertainty.
